etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 冫(bīng) -> water that is frozen -> ice
etymology.interpretation: The character 冰(bīng, "ice") combines the semantic element 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") indicating the substance and the phonetic element 冫, which provides the sound. Together they represent water that has frozen into ice.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢在夏天吃冰淇淋(我喜歡在夏天吃冰淇淋)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài xiàtiān chī bīngqílín.
examples.english: I like eating ice cream in the summer.

examples.chinese: 湖面上结了一层厚厚的冰(湖面上結了一層厚厚的冰)。
examples.pinyin: Hú miàn shàng jié le yī céng hòu hòu de bīng.
examples.english: A thick layer of ice formed on the surface of the lake.

examples.chinese: 冬天时(冬天時)，河里的水会结冰(河裡的水會結冰)。
examples.pinyin: Dōngtiān shí, hé lǐ de shuǐ huì jié bīng.
examples.english: In winter, the water in the river will freeze.
